title: piptocarpha subcorymbosa
intro: Piptocarpha subcorymbosa, also known as the Brazilian button plant, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Brazil. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and stems, which contain flavonoids and saponins with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Piptocarpha subcorymbosa include the potential treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Piptocarpha subcorymbosa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine in Brazil and other parts of South America.
